title: Nein
description: Given $k$ and $n$, find the $n$-th positive integer $x$ such that the decimal representation of $x\cdot\underbrace{999\ldots9}_k$ doesn't contain any $9$.
input: The only line contains two integers $k$ and $n$ ($1\le k\le 18$, $1\le n\le 10^{18}$).
hint: For $k = 1$, the sequence of all valid numbers starts with $2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 12, 13, \ldots$
source: Camp, Petrozavodsk Programming Camp, Summer 2021, Day 7: Moscow IPT Contest, Contest, Open Cup, 2021/2022 Season, Stage 1: Grand Prix of Dolgoprudny
